topological_discrete_sector: RP³ = S³/Z₂  +  non-trivial spin structure (T² = −I)  +  2π winding θ-term
B2_promotion: antipodal Z₂ → RP³ quotient + non-trivial spin structure
double_cover_unification: great circle on S³ (2π = closure quantum, B1) = double traversal of non-contractible RP³ loop (π, π₁ generator, B2)

tests.name: T1_antipodal_involution_Z2
tests.description: σ: p → −p is an involution (σ² = id) acting freely on S³ (no fixed points). The quotient S³/Z₂ = RP³ is a smooth manifold; the antipodal Z₂ (B2) becomes the deck transformation, a topological datum.

tests.name: T2_double_cover_closure_quantum
tests.description: Great circle on S³ (length 2π = closure quantum) = double traversal of the non-contractible RP³ loop (length π). The closure quantum (B1) and the antipodal Z₂ generator (B2) are the two faces of the S³ → RP³ double cover.

tests.name: T3_pi1_rp3_is_Z2
tests.description: π₁(RP³) = Z₂: the non-contractible RP³ loop lifts to an open S³ path (p → −p); its square lifts to a closed S³ path (p → −p → p), which is contractible since π₁(S³) = 0. Hence [γ]² = 1 in π₁(RP³) — the antipodal Z₂ is exactly the fundamental group of the quotient.

tests.name: T4_spin_structure_T2_minus_I
tests.description: T = iσ_y, T² = −I: the non-trivial (antiperiodic) spin structure on RP³. RP³ admits 2 spin structures (H¹(RP³,Z₂)=Z₂); the 4π-periodic spinor selects the non-trivial one. This is the spinor content of B2, now a topological choice rather than an imposed sign.

tests.name: T5_winding_theta_term
tests.description: Closure quantum = winding number of the phase map S¹ → U(1): ∮dφ = 2π·n. Topological total-derivative term S_top = 2π·n. Integer-quantized by single-valuedness; metric-independent (reparam-invariant). This IS B1, now a term in the action rather than an imposed constraint.

tests.name: T6_variational_consistency
tests.description: The winding θ-term is a topological total derivative: homotopic paths (same winding, same endpoints) give the same S_top. δS_top = 0 under local variations preserving winding, so the term does not modify the local EOM. The topological sector is variationally consistent with the smooth Einstein–Maxwell–Dirac–throat action.

tests.name: T7_K_Q_from_topological_sector
tests.description: K(x) = 2x/(1+x) and Q(x, c) recomputed with the closure quantum sourced by the winding θ-term (B1) and the equal split enforced by the RP³ Z₂ (B2) — both now action data. F² = K²·Q to machine precision: the throat-action results follow from the topological sector + stationary action, no longer requiring B1/B2 as separate imposed constraints.

tests.name: T8_promotion_assessment
tests.description: B1 → winding θ-term and B2 → RP³ quotient + non-trivial spin structure are both genuine topological/discrete action data. The closure quantum and antipodal Z₂ are no longer imposed constraints. Scaffold barrier count: 5 → 3.

B1_closure_quantum.topological_datum: winding number of phase map S¹ → U(1)

B2_antipodal_Z2.promoted_to: RP³ = S³/Z₂ quotient + non-trivial spin structure
B2_antipodal_Z2.topological_datum: π₁(RP³) = Z₂ deck transformation + spin structure (T²=−I)

residual_barriers.B3_boundary_conditions: hard-wall Dirichlet from T²=−I (consistent with non-trivial spin structure, but BC derivation from bulk still separate)
residual_barriers.B4_dimensional_bridge: 2π winding is metric-free, but physical length 2πR and mass scale still need m_e anchor
residual_barriers.B5_5d_to_4d_reduction: unaffected; radial reduction producing F² remains unconstructed

tests.topological_discrete_sector: RP³ = S³/Z₂  +  non-trivial spin structure (T² = −I)  +  2π winding θ-term

verdict:
PROMOTION SUCCEEDS. Both B1 (closure quantum) and B2 (antipodal Z₂) are promoted from imposed constraints to terms in a single topological/discrete action sector:

    RP³ = S³/Z₂  +  non-trivial spin structure (T² = −I)  +  2π winding θ-term

B2 → quotient + spin structure: σ is a free involution (σ² = id, no fixed points), so S³/Z₂ = RP³ is a smooth manifold with π₁(RP³) = Z₂ (the non-contractible loop squared lifts to a contractible S³ path). The spinor transport T = iσ_y with T² = −I selects the non-trivial of the two RP³ spin structures (4π-periodic / antiperiodic spinors). The antipodal Z₂ is now the deck transformation plus a spin-structure choice — topological data, not an imposed symmetry.

B1 → winding θ-term: the closure quantum is the winding number of the phase map S¹ → U(1), ∮dφ = 2π·n, a topological total-derivative term S_top = 2π·n. It is integer-quantized by single-valuedness and metric-independent (reparam-invariant), and being a total derivative it does not modify the local EOM — variationally consistent with the smooth Einstein–Maxwell–Dirac–throat action.

The two are unified by the S³ → RP³ double cover: a great circle on S³ (length 2π = closure quantum, B1) is exactly a double traversal of the non-contractible RP³ loop (length π, the π₁(RP³) = Z₂ generator, B2). With both as action data, K(x) = 2x/(1+x) and Q(x, c) — hence F² = K²·Q — follow from the topological sector + stationary action to machine precision, with the closure quantum and antipodal symmetry no longer imposed.

Scaffold barrier count reduced 5 → 3. Residual: B3 (boundary conditions), B4 (dimensional bridge — the 2π winding is metric-free but the physical scale still needs m_e), B5 (5D→4D reduction, unaffected).
